comment.body:
I was using that as an example to demonstrate the bounces. But to be technical, the first bounce after the -10.104% correction from January 3rd 2022 - January 27th 2022 (I take the closing prices).

The S&P bounced from January 27th 2022 lows to the high on 2/2 was roughly 6.7% in 4 trading day for S&P.

The point remains the same. It erased half in less than a week

The fact that we didn’t get that does not align with previous bear markets. Tells me there’s no crisis. There may not even be a crash. New lows, maybe. But I don’t think it will be extreme and I don’t think it’ll be the typical crash 

We may just get wide range of up/down/sideways the next 2 months and every narrative is useless until June/July where we can see the fall picture better

My opinion? Next week on April 2nd, tariffs are lowered by other countries and it’s a nothing burger and we finally get a V shaped. This aligns with Alma’s view on a bear trap

comment.body:
Ya but those rallies occurred after the market dropped more than it has here. For an equivalent bounce from where the market bottomed from those lows in 2022 would mean we’d need to be trading around 5330. So this bounce we just saw could still be part of the initial wave down.

But, there are some names holding onto a high lows here, like NVDA, AAPL, GE, TSLA, etc. Now that we are close to Alma’s 5567 what’s next? Also have the probability of some stabilizing news on April 2nd. 

However, Monday seems entirely probable for a an open and trade lower. Tough position right here. 2 days can make a massive difference in this environment

comment.body:
You using that bounce after the 27th of January correction helps my point even more. That bounce is similar to the 275+ point bounce we just saw. The market then went much lower after that. However, after going lower, it did a massive bear market bounce… the type of bounce I was referring to would need to occur at 5300’s to match the decline preceding it.

So what I’m saying is, 2-3 days could make a 150-250 point difference in your long entry for this type of bounce. That was my point is all.
